does: This paper documents the comprehensive safety evaluation and mitigation strategy developed before deploying deep research capabilities. The authors conduct external red teaming exercises to identify potential misuses and failure modes, apply their Preparedness Framework to assess frontier risks across multiple risk categories, and detail the specific technical and procedural mitigations implemented to address discovered vulnerabilities. The report presents findings from systematic testing of the system's behavior under adversarial conditions, risk categorization results, and documentation of safeguards integrated into the system's design, training, and deployment.
connects: This work builds on the growing field of AI safety evaluation and responsible deployment practices established by prior research on AI alignment, red teaming methodologies, and risk assessment frameworks. It extends earlier work on frontier risk evaluation by applying structured assessment approaches to research automation capabilities. The effort connects to broader conversations about responsible AI release protocols, drawing on precedents in pandemic biosecurity assessment, dual-use research evaluation, and staged deployment strategies. The Preparedness Framework referenced represents an evolution of safety evaluation practices refined through previous large model releases and collaborative work across the AI safety and governance communities.
enables: This work establishes concrete practices and empirical baselines for evaluating and safely deploying advanced research automation systems. By documenting the specific risks identified and mitigations implemented, it enables future systems to learn from these evaluations and develop more targeted safety measures. The report provides a template for assessing similar frontier capabilities, supporting the development of industry standards for responsible deployment. It also points toward open problems in measuring research capability risks, improving red teaming effectiveness for novel capabilities, and balancing capability advancement with meaningful safety assurance in high-stakes domains.
